title: cylindropuntia echinocarpa
intro: Cylindropuntia echinocarpa, also known as the Devil's Head Cholla, is a type of cactus native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a succulent plant with sharp, needle-like spines and red or green flowers that bloom from June to October. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and alleviate pain due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Cylindropuntia echinocarpa, such as tinctures and salves, can be used to support skin health and wound healing, while also providing antioxidant and antiseptic benefits.
